title: The Memory Atlas
genre: Cinematic Puzzle-Pop · Art-Rock · Orchestral Trip-Hop · Emotional Electronic Folk
concept: A cartographer's record of a childhood that can only be reached by half-remembered roads — every song a different room in a house that doesn't exist anymore, mapped from memory instead of geography.
statement: Music-box textures, accordion played for ache rather than nostalgia, and a children's choir used only as distant wordless texture — curiosity and wonder without ever tipping into kitsch.
visualIdentity: Hand-drawn atlases, pressed flowers between letter pages, attic light, paper boats, a globe with the oceans worn pale by fingers.
typographyMood: Hand-set, slightly uneven serif — like type from an old map legend. Warm, intimate, never sterile.
musicalStyle: Cinematic puzzle-pop, art-rock, orchestral trip-hop, emotional electronic folk, dreamlike DnB accents. Piano, music-box textures, accordion used emotionally, cello, soft DnB percussion, nylon guitar, warm synth pads, children's choir only as distant wordless texture.
vocalDirection: Neutral European English, intimate, curious, fragile but hopeful — a narrator piecing together their own past in real time.

emotionalArc.phase: The discovery of what was hidden
